markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has confirmed Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 console launch following multiple delays from earlier 2025 and spring 2026 targets, giving the market a clear resolution window roughly five months out. This extended timeline fuels trader focus on high-profile cultural and tech milestones—such as major album drops, AI model releases, and geopolitical developments—that could realistically occur beforehand. Industry observers note Rockstar’s pattern of prioritizing polish over rushed schedules, while the game’s massive cultural footprint continues to shape release strategies across the broader entertainment sector, with competitors already adjusting calendars around the confirmed date.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York sex-crimes retrials, including the May 2026 deadlock on the remaining rape charge, have solidified trader consensus around the No Prison Time outcome at 83.6%. The 2024 overturn of his original New York conviction and the mixed 2025 retrial verdict limited new exposure, while ongoing appeals of the consecutive 16-year California sentence—still under review by skeptical appellate panels—introduce further uncertainty. Weinstein’s documented health challenges and time already served at Rikers reinforce the market’s view that additional significant incarceration is unlikely absent successful new prosecutions. Upcoming decisions on whether prosecutors pursue another retrial or appeal outcomes will serve as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a narrow edge in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final after a 5-4 road victory in Game 1 that flipped series momentum and implied probabilities. The Knights advanced by overcoming the Colorado Avalanche in the Western Conference Final, showcasing resilience in comeback situations and strong special-teams play. Carolina Hurricanes earned home-ice advantage after a dominant regular-season performance that placed them atop the Eastern Conference standings and a five-game Eastern Conference Final win over Montreal, but the early deficit now pressures their depth and goaltending adjustments heading into Game 2. Trader consensus reflects these late-series dynamics in a closely contested matchup where either side retains realistic paths to the title.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs enter the 2026 NBA Finals as clear favorites at 64.1% implied probability, driven by their league-best 62-20 regular-season mark, Victor Wembanyama’s elite two-way dominance, and a grueling playoff path that included a seven-game Western Conference Finals upset of the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder. De’Aaron Fox and Dylan Harper have complemented the young core with strong postseason contributions, while home-court advantage for Game 1 reinforces trader consensus. The New York Knicks sit at 36.1% on the strength of their 53-29 record, an 11-game winning streak, and first Finals appearance since 1999, though Mitchell Robinson’s finger injury and the Spurs’ superior roster depth create a measurable gap in current market pricing.

eventMetadata.context_description: France, Spain, and England lead the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market because their deep squads, strong qualifying campaigns, and recent international form have established them as the clearest contenders among an expanded 48-team field. markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party as general secretary, president, and Central Military Commission chairman, with no verified challenges to his authority emerging in recent months. Official activities through early 2026, including New Year addresses outlining the 15th Five-Year Plan and sustained diplomatic engagements, underscore continuity. The 21st Party Congress, scheduled for late 2027, represents the next formal leadership review, where analysts anticipate Xi extending his tenure amid ongoing anti-corruption measures and elite reshuffles that have reinforced rather than undermined his position. This absence of disruptive events or succession signals aligns with trader consensus assigning low probability to removal before the end of 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field, absent an incumbent or dominant successor following recent cycles, has produced trader consensus behind Gavin Newsom at 25.1 percent implied probability, reflecting his California governorship, executive record, and established fundraising reach. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 9.4 percent draws support from progressive coalitions, Kamala Harris at 8.0 percent from prior national visibility, and Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro from Senate and gubernatorial bases respectively. The long tail of lower-priced outcomes underscores limited early consolidation. Performance in the 2026 midterms, primary results in key states, and turnout patterns among core Democratic voting blocs could narrow the field ahead of formal announcements or endorsements.
